original_prompt:
I'll be arriving in London on March 15th. Last year, I stayed at the 'London Luxury Hotel', which had amazing reviews and ratings. It was super quiet and clean, and my room had a view of the Thames River. It was such a great experience! This time, I want to try out a new hotel. Once I figure out which one is the best, I'll head there and then find a good restaurant to eat at with my friends.
I have a budget of 160 for the hotel, and I want to stay in a hotel with the highest rating. Can you recommend a hotel? Tell me the name, rating, price range, and address. Thanks a bunch!
assessment_reasoning: The prompt clearly states the main objective: recommend a hotel in London within a budget of 160 and give its name, rating, price range, and address, and to pick the hotel with the highest rating. That makes the primary requirements explicit. Minor ambiguities remain, however: it doesn’t state whether the budget is per night or total, or which currency to assume; it doesn’t specify tie-breaking rules if multiple hotels share the top rating (cheapest, closest to Thames, etc.); it omits stay length, number of guests/rooms, or exact check-in/check-out needs; and it’s unclear whether the user’s past preferences (quiet, river view, very clean) should influence the recommendation. Also the prompt doesn’t clarify whether the budget comparison should be inclusive (<=160) or strict (<160). These issues are relatively small and don’t prevent completing the task, so the prompt is slightly ambiguous.

variations.L1: I'm heading to London soon and need a place to stay. Got any hotel suggestions?
variations.L2: I'm arriving in London on March 15th and want a new hotel to try. I have a budget of 160. Can you recommend a hotel and tell me its name, rating, price range, and address?
variations.L3: I'll be in London on March 15. I'd like a hotel that costs no more than £160 per night and has the highest possible rating within that budget. Recommend one hotel and give me the hotel's name, rating, price_min and price_max (per night), and full address. If there are ties, pick one.
variations.L4: I'll be arriving in London on March 15th. Last year I stayed at 'London Luxury Hotel' and loved it. This time I want to try a different place. I have a budget of 160 (per night) and I'd like the hotel with the highest rating that fits that budget. Can you recommend a hotel and provide the name, rating, price range (min–max), and full address? Thanks!
variations.L5: I arrive in London on March 15. Please programmatically find the top-rated hotel in London whose price_max is less than 160 GBP per night and return its details. Steps: (1) call get_all_hotels_in_city(city='London') to get candidates, (2) call get_hotels_prices(hotel_names=[...]) to obtain price_min/price_max, (3) filter to hotels with price_max < 160, (4) call get_rating_reviews_for_hotels(hotel_names=[filtered...]) to get ratings, (5) select the hotel(s) with the maximum rating (if multiple, return the first), (6) call get_hotels_address(hotel_name=chosen_name) for the address. Return the final result in plain text with these fields labeled exactly: Name:, Rating:, Price_min:, Price_max:, Address:. All prices should be GBP per night.
